Subject: History_Questions
Question: True or False: Cleopatra was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt.
Response1:
Explanation:
Cleopatra VII Philopator was indeed the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt. She reigned from 51 BC to her death in 30 BC after being defeated by Roman forces led by Mark Antony and later Octavian (later known as Augustus).

Final answer: True

Response3:
Explanation: Cleopatra VII was indeed the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt. She reigned from 51 BC to 30 BC.

Final Answer: True

Response6:
False
Cleopatra was not the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom. The kingdom continued to exist until 30 BC when it was conquered by Rome under Julius Caesar and Mark Antony. Cleopatra VII ruled from around 51-30 BC, making her one of the final rulers but not the very last.
